# Tailwind CSS in Dioxus

dx-cli (v0.6+) compiles Tailwind v4 internally — no Node.js, npm, or `package.json` needed.

## File Structure

```
app/
  tailwind.css          ← INPUT — commit this to git
  assets/
    tailwind.css        ← OUTPUT — compiled by dx, add to .gitignore
```

Add the compiled output to `.gitignore`:

```gitignore
# Tailwind compiled output — regenerated by dx serve / dx build
app/assets/tailwind.css
```

## tailwind.css (Input File)

Minimal setup — no preflight, only theme + utilities:

```css
@layer theme, base, components, utilities;
@import "tailwindcss/theme.css" layer(theme);
@import "tailwindcss/utilities.css" layer(utilities);

/* Scan Rust source for class: "..." utility strings */
@source "./src/**/*.rs";
```

If you need the full reset (new projects with no existing CSS):

```css
@import "tailwindcss";

@source "./src/**/*.rs";
```

## Linking the Compiled CSS

Reference the compiled output via `asset!()` in `assets.rs` and load it in `App`:

```rust
// src/assets.rs
pub const TAILWIND_CSS: Asset = asset!("/assets/tailwind.css");

// src/app.rs
#[component]
pub fn App() -> Element {
    rsx! {
        document::Stylesheet { href: MAIN_CSS }
        document::Stylesheet { href: TAILWIND_CSS }
        Router::<Route> {}
    }
}
```

## Dioxus.toml

No special Tailwind config required. dx auto-discovers `tailwind.css` in the crate root:

```toml
[application]
name = "my-app"

[web.app]
title = "My App"

[web.resource]
style = []
```

## Dark / Light Theme Variables

Uses the **CSS Space Toggle** pattern. Each token is defined once with both values inline; the `--dark` / `--light` toggles activate the correct one. `prefers-color-scheme` is respected automatically, and an explicit `[data-theme]` attribute overrides it.

### 1. Toggle setup (in `assets/main.css`)

```css
/* Tailwind custom variant — enables class: "light:text-fg" etc. */
@custom-variant light (&:where([data-theme=light], [data-theme=light] *));

/* Space Toggle — dark is default.
   Pattern: var(--dark, <dark-val>) var(--light, <light-val>)
   `initial` = "on", empty string = "off" */
html[data-theme="dark"]  { --dark: initial; --light: ; }
html[data-theme="light"] { --dark: ;        --light: initial; }

@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)  { :root { --dark: initial; --light: ; } }
@media (prefers-color-scheme: light) { :root { --dark: ;        --light: initial; } }
```

### 2. Token definitions

Each variable holds both values simultaneously — the active toggle selects one:

```css
:root {
  /* Backgrounds */
  --app-bg:            var(--dark, #0A0F1C)           var(--light, #FFFFFF);
  --app-bg-alt:        var(--dark, #141B2D)           var(--light, #F7F9FC);
  --app-bg-tint:       var(--dark, #1A2332)           var(--light, #EEF2F8);
  --app-surface:       var(--dark, #141B2D)           var(--light, #FFFFFF);

  /* Foregrounds */
  --app-fg:            var(--dark, #D4DBEA)           var(--light, #1A2741);
  --app-fg-muted:      var(--dark, #8B9AAF)           var(--light, #6B7894);
  --app-fg-faint:      var(--dark, #5D6985)           var(--light, #9AA6BF);

  /* Borders */
  --app-border:        var(--dark, #232B3D)           var(--light, #E4E8F0);
  --app-border-strong: var(--dark, #3E4A60)           var(--light, #C9D2E0);

  /* Accent */
  --app-accent:        var(--dark, #1663EF)           var(--light, #002060);
  --app-accent-hover:  var(--dark, #054DBA)           var(--light, #001540);
  --app-accent-fg:     var(--dark, #FFFFFF)           var(--light, #FFFFFF);

  /* Brand */
  --app-blue:          var(--dark, #4F8DFF)           var(--light, #1663EF);
  --app-blue-tint:     var(--dark, rgba(22,99,239,.18)) var(--light, #DAE3F3);
}
```

### 3. Switch themes from Rust

```rust
fn set_theme(dark: bool) {
    let theme = if dark { "dark" } else { "light" };
    web_sys::window()
        .unwrap()
        .document()
        .unwrap()
        .document_element()
        .unwrap()
        .set_attribute("data-theme", theme)
        .ok();
}
```

## Custom Design Tokens

Bridge the CSS variables above into Tailwind with `@theme inline` so utilities like `bg-bg` and `text-fg` emit `var(--app-*)` directly and stay reactive to the `html[data-theme]` switch:

```css
@layer theme, base, components, utilities;
@import "tailwindcss/theme.css" layer(theme);
@import "tailwindcss/utilities.css" layer(utilities);

@source "./src/**/*.rs";

@theme inline {
  --color-bg:            var(--app-bg);
  --color-bg-alt:        var(--app-bg-alt);
  --color-bg-tint:       var(--app-bg-tint);
  --color-surface:       var(--app-surface);
  --color-fg:            var(--app-fg);
  --color-fg-muted:      var(--app-fg-muted);
  --color-fg-faint:      var(--app-fg-faint);
  --color-border:        var(--app-border);
  --color-border-strong: var(--app-border-strong);
  --color-accent:        var(--app-accent);
  --color-accent-hover:  var(--app-accent-hover);
  --color-accent-fg:     var(--app-accent-fg);
  --color-blue:          var(--app-blue);
  --color-blue-tint:     var(--app-blue-tint);
}
```

## Usage in Rust Components

```rust
rsx! {
    div { class: "flex flex-col gap-4 bg-bg text-fg",
        button { class: "rounded bg-accent text-white px-4 py-2 hover:bg-accent-hover",
            "Click me"
        }
    }
}
```

## What Goes in Git

| File | Commit? |
|------|---------|
| `app/tailwind.css` | Yes — source of truth |
| `app/assets/tailwind.css` | **No** — gitignore it |
| `package.json` / `node_modules` | **No** — not needed |
